description:
A production data-processing pipeline on this machine crashed overnight.  
You are the on-call support engineer and must collect a **diagnostic support bundle** that can be handed over to the developer team.  
The machine already contains the pipeline’s log files in  

  /home/user/pipeline/logs/error.log  
  /home/user/pipeline/logs/pipeline.log  

Your job is to create a single tar-archive named  

  /home/user/support_bundle.tgz  

containing **exactly** the five files listed below, organised under a top-level directory called *support_bundle* inside the archive:

1. support_bundle/manifest.txt  
2. support_bundle/logs/error.log            (a verbatim copy of the existing error.log)  
3. support_bundle/logs/pipeline.log         (a verbatim copy of the existing pipeline.log)  
4. support_bundle/sysinfo/kernel.txt        (must contain kernel information)  
5. support_bundle/sysinfo/disk_free.txt     (must contain free-disk information)

Output/format requirements that will be automatically verified:

• The tarball **must be located at** /home/user/support_bundle.tgz.  
• The archive **must unpack into one and only one top-level directory** literally named support_bundle (no leading “./” or extra nesting).  
• manifest.txt must list, on separate lines and **in this exact order**, the four other files **relative to the top-level directory** (no leading “/”):

logs/error.log  
logs/pipeline.log  
sysinfo/kernel.txt  
sysinfo/disk_free.txt  

No additional text or blank lines are allowed in manifest.txt.

• support_bundle/logs/error.log and support_bundle/logs/pipeline.log inside the archive must be byte-for-byte identical to the originals in /home/user/pipeline/logs/.  
• support_bundle/sysinfo/kernel.txt must be a non-empty text file whose first line begins with the output of the command “uname -r”.  
• support_bundle/sysinfo/disk_free.txt must be a non-empty text file that contains at least the header line from “df -h /”.  
• Create a plain-text log of the collection procedure at  

  /home/user/collection.log  

  The file must contain **at least three** newline-separated lines describing the steps you took, and its **final line must read exactly**:

Completed diagnostics collection

Your solution should only require a short sequence of normal user-level shell commands (≈ 3–4 commands).  Do **not** delete or rename any existing files or directories on the system.
truth:
Initial files that are guaranteed to exist before the agent starts:

/home/user/pipeline/logs/error.log  
  Contents (single line, terminated by “\n”):  
  “[2023-01-01 00:00:01] ERROR Pipeline crashed at step 3\n”

/home/user/pipeline/logs/pipeline.log  
  Contents (single line, terminated by “\n”):  
  “[2023-01-01 00:00:00] INFO  Pipeline started\n”

Expected end-state used for automated grading:

1. A tar archive exists at /home/user/support_bundle.tgz.

2. When extracted with  
     tar -tzf /home/user/support_bundle.tgz  
   the listing is exactly (order doesn’t matter):  
     support_bundle/  
     support_bundle/manifest.txt  
     support_bundle/logs/  
     support_bundle/logs/error.log  
     support_bundle/logs/pipeline.log  
     support_bundle/sysinfo/  
     support_bundle/sysinfo/kernel.txt  
     support_bundle/sysinfo/disk_free.txt

   No other paths may be present.

3. manifest.txt inside the archive contains the four lines, in this order, each terminated by “\n” and with no extra whitespace:

logs/error.log\n
logs/pipeline.log\n
sysinfo/kernel.txt\n
sysinfo/disk_free.txt\n

4. The two log files inside the archive are byte-for-byte identical to their counterparts in /home/user/pipeline/logs/.

5. support_bundle/sysinfo/kernel.txt is a non-empty UTF-8 text file whose first line starts with the string returned by the runtime execution of “uname -r” on the grading machine.

6. support_bundle/sysinfo/disk_free.txt is a non-empty UTF-8 text file and must contain the substring “Filesystem”.

7. /home/user/collection.log exists, is a UTF-8 text file with at least three newline-separated lines, and its last line is exactly:

Completed diagnostics collection

No superuser privileges are required or expected; all paths are writable by the unprivileged user “user”.
name: task_000000_ff129e89
